does: This paper reports on a direct comparative alignment evaluation exercise where Anthropic and OpenAI simultaneously assessed each other's large language models using their own established evaluation frameworks and methodologies. The study documents concrete findings from applying alignment assessment tools to state-of-the-art models, examining how different evaluation approaches capture model behavior and safety properties. Rather than proposing new methods, the work delivers empirical results from this rare cross-organizational evaluation effort, offering insights into which alignment properties can be reliably measured and where different evaluation frameworks agree or diverge on model performance.
connects: This work extends the emerging field of AI safety evaluation by building on prior alignment assessment frameworks developed independently by both organizations. It connects to previous research on red-teaming, behavioral testing of language models, and formal safety evaluation protocols. The paper responds to open questions about whether different alignment research programs converge on similar safety conclusions and whether evaluation methods remain consistent across different model architectures and training approaches. It also engages with broader conversations about transparency and benchmarking standards in AI safety research.
enables: This comparative evaluation exercise establishes foundations for future cross-organization alignment research and collaborative safety standards. By documenting where evaluation frameworks produce consistent versus divergent results, the work enables development of more robust, generalizable alignment benchmarks that transcend individual research programs. The findings point toward establishing industry-wide alignment assessment protocols and shared benchmarks for measuring model safety. It also supports the possibility of independent safety verification and creates a template for how competing AI labs might cooperatively advance alignment research while maintaining appropriate safeguards.
